seoTitle: Leonay vs Leppington — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Leonay and Leppington. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Leonay and Leppington.
heading: Leonay vs Leppington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Leonay, New South Wales and Leppington, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Leppington has a lower overall climate risk score (44/100) compared to Leonay (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Leonay or Leppington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Leonay has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Leppington scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Leonay scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Leppington scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Leonay or Leppington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Leppington has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
